title: SysBio FAIRplex Policies
body:
Expectations For SysBio FAIRplex Site Users

  • Users will adhere to the data use requirements as implemented by each AMP Project (See how to register for and access AMP data)
  • Authors citing the results of analyses performed using SysBio FAIRplex must cite: a) SysBio FAIRplex, b) all external programs that collected the donors and data underlying each dataset and their funders, and c) the relevant publication(s) underlying the results accessed via SysBio FAIRplex (see “How to Cite SysBio FAIRplex” below).
  • Per the AMPs and the Requirements for National Institutes of Health Controlled-Access Data Repositories and Users, users must report all suspected data incidents to the SysBio Data Access Committee through support@sysbio-fairplex.org and to the NIH Incident Response Team. NIH Incident Response Team notifications can be made by phone (301) 496-HELP (4357); Toll Free Number: (866) 319-4357 or TTY: (301) 496-8294, and can also be sent by email to NIHInfoSec@nih.gov or via the Report an Incident Link: https://irtportal.ocio.nih.gov/. For the NIH Data Management Incident Notification inbox, email DMI_OER@mail.nih.gov.

Agreeing to these provisions is a requirement of SysBio FAIRplex site use.

Reusing SysBio FAIRplex Content

Users are permitted to use figures and screenshots from SysBio FAIRplex in presentations and publications, with proper citation (see above and below).

User Tracking and Privacy Policy

This website is committed to protecting your privacy. We do not collect personally identifiable information such as names, email addresses, or account details. However, we may collect limited, non-identifiable information through the use of cookies and similar technologies, including pages visited, time spent on pages, browser type, device information, and general geographic region (e.g., country or city). This information is aggregated and does not identify individual users. We use cookies to understand how visitors interact with the site, improve site performance and user experience, and analyze general usage patterns. Cookies are small text files stored on your device, and you can control or disable them through your browser settings. By continuing to use this site, you consent to this use. The information collected is used solely to monitor and improve website functionality, understand usage trends, and support the development of new features. We do not sell or share your data with third parties for marketing or use it to identify individual users. We may use third-party analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics or similar services) that collect anonymized usage data and may use their own cookies, governed by their respective privacy policies. We take reasonable measures to protect the information collected, although no method of internet transmission is completely secure. You may disable cookies or use browser tools to limit tracking, though this may affect site functionality. We may update this Privacy Policy periodically, with changes posted on this page. We welcome any questions at support@sysbio-fairplex.org.

Resource Distribution and Sharing

To encourage data sharing and reproducibility, the FAIRplex public portal (sysbio-fairplex.org) surfaces vetted summary-level data and dataset-level metadata. We are producing open-source methods and analysis workflows. We make these freely available for use by any other parties; the code is supplied “as is” with no implied warranty or promises of support. Code should not be reused for harm. We maintain GitHub repositories from which interested parties can download the source code.

How to Cite SysBio FAIRplex

Users must appropriately cite and/or acknowledge SysBio FAIRplex and all the relevant AMP programs as follows:

The results published here are in whole or in part based on data derivatives from [Source study name and DOI] supported by [NIH grants] obtained from [AMP/Portal]. The data derivatives were generated by the SysBio FAIRplex Team funded through the NIH Common Fund Award Number OT2OD037975 as part of the Common Fund’s Venture Program SysBio Initiative.

Users who cite ENRICHR pathway analyses on the Gene Expression Browser should also cite the following additional references:

  • https://doi.org/10.1186/1471-2105-14-128
  • https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkw377
  • https://doi.org/10.1002/cpz1.90
